Skinnytaste > Main Ingredient > The Best Chicken Breast Recipes > Grilled Chicken with Spinach and Melted Mozzarella

Grilled Chicken with Spinach and Melted Mozzarella

195 Cals 31 Protein 3.5 Carbs 6 Fats 141
4.85 from 19 votes
19748 shares
Jump to Recipe
Save ItSaved!
This post may contain affiliate links. Read my disclosure policy.

Grilled chicken topped with spinach, melted mozzarella and roasted peppers – a quick and easy chicken dish your family will love!

Grilled Chicken with Spinach and Mozzarella

This chicken breast recipe is made with satuteed garlicky spinach and melted mozzarella. Perfect for those hot days, no need to turn your oven on, you can do this all outside on the grill! A few more grilled chicken recipes I love are Grilled Chicken Shawarma, Korean Grilled Chicken and Honey Balsamic Grilled Chicken and Vegetables.

What is the best way to grill chicken?

If you’re doing this on the grill, make sure to have the sauteed spinach and peppers ready. First grease the rack with non stick spray or oil. Then turn on the grill on and let the grill (or grill pan) get hot. Once it’s hot, place the chicken breasts and let stand until there are grill marks and chicken has browned. Then flip and do the same. This is anywhere between 2-5 minutes per side depending on it’ thickness. You can turn the grill off, top with sauteed spinach and mozzarella and let stand for a minute or 2 with the grill closed to let the cheese melt.

You can make this with skinless chicken thighs as well. Just trim the fat and adjust cooking time, thighs take a bit longer.

More Grilled Chicken Recipes You Might Enjoy:

  • Grilled Chicken Avocado Mango Salad
  • Grilled Chicken Summer Salad with Strawberries, Spinach and Feta
  • Korean Grilled Chicken
  • Grilled Chicken Tacos with Lettuce Slaw and Cotija Cheese
  • Houston’s Grilled Chicken Salad with Honey Lime Vinaigrette
Print WW Personal Points
4.85 from 19 votes
Did you make this recipe? Leave a review »

Grilled Chicken with Spinach and Melted Mozzarella

195 Cals 31 Protein 3.5 Carbs 6 Fats
Prep Time: 2 mins
Cook Time: 15 mins
Total Time: 17 mins
Yield: 6 Servings
COURSE: Dinner
CUISINE: Italian
Grilled chicken topped with sauteed garlicky spinach, mozzarella and roasted peppers – a quick and easy chicken dish your family will love!

Ingredients

  • 24 oz 3 large chicken breasts sliced in half lengthwise to make 6
  • kosher salt and pepper to taste
  • 1 tsp olive oil
  • 3 cloves garlic, crushed
  • 10 oz frozen spinach, drained
  • 3 oz shredded part skim mozzarella
  • 1/2 cup roasted red pepper, sliced in strips (packed in water)
  • olive oil spray
Save

Instructions

  • Preheat oven to 400°F. Season chicken with salt and pepper. Lightly spray a grill or grill pan with oil. Cook chicken until no longer pink, about 2 to 3 minutes per side.
  • Heat a skillet over medium heat. Add oil and garlic, sauté a 30 seconds, add spinach, salt and pepper. Cook until heated through, 2 to 3 minutes.
  • Place chicken on a baking sheet, divide spinach evenly between the 6 pieces and place on top. Top each with 1/2 oz mozzarella, roasted peppers and bake until melted, about 3 minutes.

Nutrition

Serving: 1piece, Calories: 195kcal, Carbohydrates: 3.5g, Protein: 31g, Fat: 6g, Saturated Fat: 2g, Cholesterol: 91mg, Sodium: 183mg, Fiber: 1.5g, Sugar: 0.5g
WW Points Plus: 5
Keywords: chicken breast recipes, grilled chicken, grilled chicken breast, grilled chicken recipe, Grilled Chicken with Spinach and Melted Mozzarella
1 SP 1 SP 3 SP 4 PP Chicken Recipes Dinner Recipes Egg Free Recipes Grilling Recipes Italian Inspired Recipes Recipes Spinach Recipes Spring Summer The Best Chicken Breast Recipes
posted July 11, 2020 by Gina
141 Comments / Leave a Comment »

Rate this Recipe:




141 comments

  1. KG — June 21, 2022 @ 7:11 pm Reply

    So easy to make and so delicious! Used fresh spinach and garlic peppers and it came out perfect. Definitely making again! Thanks Gina! 

  2. Jamie — January 11, 2022 @ 9:07 pm Reply

    So I loved the flavor of this chicken. I ended up using fresh spinach and next time need to use more. I also just oven baked it for 15min at 400 and then layered with the toppings for another 5 min. They were so juicy. 

  3. Chuck Kanupp — July 29, 2021 @ 7:03 pm Reply

    This a beautiful dish. I smoked the roasted peppers on the grill, while I grilled the chicken, and paired it with your “Perfectly Grilled Zucchini” as well. My wife adored the meal.

  4. Frances — April 19, 2021 @ 4:00 pm Reply

    This is a favorite recipe. Can’t believe I just made it for the first time. I used grilled chicken that I had marinated lemon, and rosemary and herbs. I used fresh spinach and also added a 1/3 cup of onion to that. Got some local Caputo Brother fresh mozzerella to splurge on these and it was so good. A winner, would be enjoyed by company. Saw a recipe in Skinny taste book for pearl couscous with veggies–also saw on on the web site, and had a version of that with this–aspargus, zucchini yellow squash, (in the couscous) Thanks for a great recipe

  5. Samantha — December 29, 2020 @ 8:06 pm Reply

    Simple and delicious! 

  6. Sue Raymond — September 26, 2020 @ 7:02 pm Reply

    This is absolutely wonderful! I used fresh spinach and the smell of the garlic cooking – wonderful. These are very flavorful and a far cry from most recipes that tend to dry the chicken out. Thank you!

  7. Mary Beth Tingley — August 11, 2020 @ 8:19 pm Reply

    Flavorful weeknight recipe! I served with leftover roasted veggies on the side, but could easily be served alongside a simple salad and steamed veggie noodles.

  8. Suzanne Knight — July 24, 2020 @ 5:32 pm Reply

    I used fresh spinach and roasted garlic (I had time…quarentine) and added a small dash of nutmeg at the end. Delish!

  9. Nick — July 22, 2020 @ 6:17 am Reply

    I’ll be honest, I had my doubts when making this but it’s truly delicious – reminded me of a Mediterranean pizza 🙂 I grilled the chicken on the BBQ and finished melting the toppings in the oven (my chicken wasn’t exactly uniform 😉 Thanks for a great (and easy!) recipe Gina.

  10. Kjerstin — July 21, 2020 @ 9:07 pm Reply

    Do you mean thawed frozen spinach? I used frozen spinach- so I couldn’t drain it? And it took longer to cook on the stove. 

    • Gina — July 31, 2020 @ 8:45 am Reply

      Yes when you thaw, you squeeze the liquid after.

  11. Cheryl A Erlandson — July 20, 2020 @ 8:56 pm Reply

    I have had many fabulous meals from the Skinny Taste website; this was not one! I read the reviews and go from that. I do not know how anyone could give this a 5 star rating. This was all right, but I certainly won’t make it again. And, yes, I followed the recipe.

  12. Shelley Ascherl — July 20, 2020 @ 5:56 pm Reply

    Cooked spinach with anything is usually not my favorite, but it was perfect in this recipe. Loved it!

  13. adrienne — July 18, 2020 @ 8:12 am Reply

    My family devoured this!  I subbed kale for spinach and used tomato confit (tomatoes cooked in olive oil and garlic) in place of the roasted red pepper because it is what I had on hand.  It was a delight watching my kids shoveling those greens in their mouths!  

    • Gina — July 18, 2020 @ 9:54 am Reply

      I’m so glad!

  14. Barbara Miller Baum — July 16, 2020 @ 3:51 pm Reply

    Hi! When I calculate the nutritional information on my WW app, one serving comes up as 3, but I’m on blue. Should I count it as 3?

    • Gina — July 20, 2020 @ 8:44 am Reply

      Its 1 on blue. Use recipe builder.

      • Monica — February 22, 2021 @ 12:36 am

        It came to 4 points when I used recipe builder. 

  15. Cathi — May 11, 2018 @ 8:25 pm Reply

    Great recipe, hubby and I loved it, only change was that I sautéed fresh spinach instead of using frozen spinach

  16. Lori Hallford — November 6, 2015 @ 1:05 am Reply

    Family loved it! Thanks for another great recipe.

  17. Nancy — July 28, 2015 @ 10:17 pm Reply

    Is it possible to do this entirely on the grill? My AC is broken and it's too hot to turn on the oven.

    • Gina — July 13, 2020 @ 1:16 pm Reply

      Sure!

  18. Unknown — June 5, 2015 @ 3:00 am Reply

    I used fresh spinach. It was delicious!

    • Gina — July 13, 2020 @ 1:16 pm Reply

      Great!

  19. Char — April 27, 2015 @ 2:53 am Reply

    I have the same question, can you use fresh spinach in lieu of frozen? Thanks!

    • Gina — July 13, 2020 @ 1:17 pm Reply

      Sure! Just wilt it first

  20. Anonymous — April 22, 2015 @ 3:21 am Reply

    Can you use fresh spinach leaves instead of frozen spinach?

  21. Anonymous — February 11, 2015 @ 6:24 am Reply

    Making it today for our company canteen (about 100 people)

  22. Emily — December 22, 2014 @ 7:42 pm Reply

    what side dish do you recommend?

  23. Aileen — June 5, 2014 @ 1:39 am Reply

    I made this the other night and everyone loved it. Thank you so much for the delicious and healthy recipes!!

  24. Anonymous — February 6, 2014 @ 6:05 pm Reply

    this is delicious. I tweaked a bit by baking thin chicken cutlets for about 5 minutes. Then adding the toppings and finishing until cheese melted. Used a very yummy roasted red pepper and artichoke bruschetta from Trader Joe's (zero points).

  25. SFT — January 4, 2014 @ 2:56 am Reply

    Made this tonight for dinner…..Loved it!! Thanks for the recipe

  26. Becky — December 9, 2013 @ 12:40 am Reply

    For those of you who've made it, what did you serve it with? Open to suggestions!

    • Dianne — August 17, 2016 @ 1:16 pm Reply

      I served it with balsamic grilled carrots and lime cilantro rice!

  27. Joy — September 28, 2013 @ 12:13 am Reply

    I made this last night and loved it so much that it's currently in the oven for dinner again! Thank you, Gina, you haven't steered me wrong yet!

  28. Miss Ralphie — April 28, 2013 @ 2:31 am Reply

    My husband and I have been trying to become healthier people since finding out that our parents are each having cholesterol, blood pressure, and heart problems early in their lives. We've even somehow managed to cut out canned vegetables, broths, and soups (they are extremely high in sodium and you usually add even MORE salt when cooking them). It has been such a challenge to find a variety of healthy recipes that weren't bland and left much to be desired. I made this tonight and we LOVED IT! I used red bell peppers instead of red peppers because Kroger had them on sale lol I think next time I'll add a little lemon juice to the spinach for a slight zing. Thank you for making my life a little easier. This was nutritious AND delicious! Even our 16 month old ate his entire plate!

  29. Unknown — April 5, 2013 @ 10:56 pm Reply

    I made this tonight. I used sweet peppers that I cooked with the spinach instead of roasted red peppers. I also served it over a bed of quinoa. Super yummy!

  30. Paty — March 21, 2013 @ 3:41 pm Reply

    We made this last night and it was FABULOUS and so easy. Thank you the entire family LOVED it.

  31. Whit — March 3, 2013 @ 11:57 pm Reply

    Wonderful and so simple! I used fresh baby spinach and wilted it in the sauté pan. Thanks Gina!

  32. Danielle — February 20, 2013 @ 1:44 am Reply

    This was awesome! The only thing I did to save on dishes was bake the whole thing for 20 minutes at 400. Served it with you asparagus and dijon vinegarett!

    • Unknown — April 10, 2013 @ 12:06 am Reply

      Awesome idea! I don't have a grill, but really want to try this recipe out. I was thinking about sauteeing the chicken breast stovetop. I got a giant bag of baby kale from Costco, gonna be an awesome sub for the spinach!

  33. Anonymous — January 28, 2013 @ 11:20 pm Reply

    I'm obsessed with all of your recipes! I recently joined Weight Watchers, and using your delicious recipes as a guide to my meal plans is fantastic. I can't wait to add this one to the list! Thank you!!!

  34. Anonymous — January 21, 2013 @ 12:14 am Reply

    Made this tonight, but marinated the chicken in fat-free Italian dressing beforehand. Came out super flavorful and moist. Thanks so much!

  35. Ashley Zacher — January 18, 2013 @ 12:23 am Reply

    I didn't have roasted red peppers, so I used tomatoes instead, with fresh mozzarella.. Delicious recipe, thank you!!

  36. Lindsey — January 13, 2013 @ 11:17 pm Reply

    I must tell you this was the first time i looked and cooked from your site and this was AMAZING!!! My 9 yr. Old daughter turns her nose on anything healthy and she couldn't get enough of it! Thank you for such a great site!

  37. Bren A- Bellflower CA — January 13, 2013 @ 5:48 pm Reply

    My kids love spinach and they loved this dish served it with a side of black bean corn salad and a delicious well balanced meal.

  38. Holly Gillespie — January 12, 2013 @ 5:41 am Reply

    I made this the other night and it came out fantastic! I did modify a few things, instead of using chicken breasts I used chicken thighs. Instead of using roasted red peppers I used sun-dried tomatoes (I'm so glad I did this!). And the leftovers heated up really nicely.

  39. Jessica — December 12, 2012 @ 11:50 pm Reply

    I did this with Fresh baby spinach. The spinach gets such a yummy garlic flavor. We also didn't have any mozzarella cheese but I thought we did and I used Swiss instead and it was actually pretty tasty!

  40. Kelly Benson-Martell — October 25, 2012 @ 3:24 am Reply

    This recipe is a great base to help me clean out the fridge. I used turkey cutlets and caramelized onions. It did increase the calories a bit but I'm sure it will still be delicious!

  41. Jenn — September 19, 2012 @ 1:26 pm Reply

    I know I should've gotten that bunch of spinach at the market. This looks delicious and healthy!

  42. Anonymous — August 22, 2012 @ 11:20 pm Reply

    can this be made with fresh spinach as well

  43. Cindy — August 2, 2012 @ 2:05 am Reply

    Made this for dinner tonight. HUGE hit with mother-in-law, husband, teenage nephew, elementary age kids and toddler! Thanks, Gina. This is going to become a staple in this household!

  44. Anonymous — July 18, 2012 @ 10:09 pm Reply

    I just made these tonight for the first time. They were amazing! The flavors were so wonderful!
    I wasn't able to grill them outside so I just did a quick oven-cooking. Even like that, they were fabulous! Thanks for all these wonderful recipies! I can't wait to try more :o)

  45. Lindsay — July 1, 2012 @ 2:31 pm Reply

    Hi – do you think that I can put the toppings on the chicken then put it back on the grill to melt instead?

  46. Tiffany K. — June 28, 2012 @ 10:42 pm Reply

    Had this tonight with your cauliflower fritters. Just so yummy!! Thanks for all these wonderful recipes. You've saved me (and my waistline) for sure 🙂

  47. Anonymous — June 26, 2012 @ 2:43 am Reply

    Made this tonight, I was looking for a new way to prepare chicken breasts and this was perfect. I love spinach so I thought this was great, and my husband loved it too. I can't wait to eat the leftovers for lunch tomorrow!

  48. Car Mama — May 21, 2012 @ 11:13 pm Reply

    Made this over the weekend & kids loved it. We all loved it. But kids had 2nds, so didn't have leftovers! Served with simple pasta (bowtie) salad with fresh tomatoes & vinaigrette.

  49. Anonymous — May 9, 2012 @ 11:23 am Reply

    Made this for dinner last night 5 star reveiws my father even liked it and he doesn't like anything

  50. Anonymous — May 3, 2012 @ 9:49 pm Reply

    Made this for dinner tonight. Too cold to grill so I pan seared the chicken breast. Can't wait to eat it.

  51. Monica — April 24, 2012 @ 2:09 am Reply

    Just made this! I had Green Giant Lightly Sauced Spinach Steamers. I thawed in microwave then sauteed with the garlic. I had a bit of left over shredded chicken. I placed it at the bottom of a dish, put the spinach on top and topped with Kraft Swiss cheese singles. It was yummy! I have 3 toddlers in the house who loved this over some rice. I had mine over salad.

  52. Nicole K — April 17, 2012 @ 8:49 pm Reply

    Made this last night and it was AWESOME…substituted fresh spinach for frozen and goat cheese in place of mozzarella….BIG hit

  53. Anonymous — April 17, 2012 @ 1:43 am Reply

    This was delicious but I got 6 pts+ on my calculator… (from Arlington VA)

  54. Anonymous — March 21, 2012 @ 11:26 pm Reply

    Made this twice now (first with froz spinach then with fresh– we preferred the fresh). However my question is with your Carbs calculation. You say it's 4 carbs here, but once I input it in MyFitnessPal's calculated, it told me 20 per serving.

    ALB

    • Gina @ Skinnytaste — May 22, 2012 @ 8:47 pm Reply

      hmm, let me recheck this

  55. Anonymous — February 27, 2012 @ 11:14 pm Reply

    I made this tonight for dinner using broccoli rabe instead of spinach and it was delicious!!!!!!!!!!
    How do you roast peppers yourself?

    • Anonymous — March 22, 2012 @ 6:07 pm Reply

      The way we roast our own peppers, is to put them on a baking sheet in the broiler until charred, blistered and black (you could do it on a grill also). Once they are out of the flame, put them in a ziploc bag for about 20 minutes. The skin will peel right off, and you have homemade roasted peppers!

  56. Anonymous — February 20, 2012 @ 3:55 pm Reply

    We loved this but the chicken was a bit dry so we have decided that we will marinate them in low cal italian dressing first. My first time making/eating fresh roaster red peppers – AWESOME!!

  57. Anonymous — February 13, 2012 @ 6:15 pm Reply

    I made this yesterday, super easy and delicious! I didn't have time to prepare the roasted red peppers so I improvised with sun-dried tomatoes and it turned out awesome!

  58. Katie @ OhShineOn — February 3, 2012 @ 1:59 am Reply

    I made this tonight with a few changes and it turned out phenomenal. It was a huge hit with my friends and it was so easy… definitely making again!

  59. Anonymous — January 13, 2012 @ 6:18 am Reply

    A friend just showed me your website today! So on my way home i picked up some chicken breast and whipped this up! WOW is all i have to say! delicious!!! It is amazing that you do all this!! thanks you SO much! i will definetly be making more of your recipes!! :):)

  60. Angie — January 10, 2012 @ 1:45 am Reply

    Super excited to try this meal. It looks great and its healthy.

  61. Maria B — December 5, 2011 @ 1:17 am Reply

    New to site. Made this for dinner tonight. It was wonderful. My family and I loved it! I only had feta cheese, so I made it with that. Definitely a keeper. Look forward to trying other recipes.

  62. Anonymous — November 19, 2011 @ 6:31 pm Reply

    Made it for lunch today. For the first time EVER my chicken breasts came out soft and juicy. It was amazingly delicious and fast. Gina, thanks again. And the cauliflower spagetti was soooo good too.

  63. Darci VC — October 26, 2011 @ 4:31 pm Reply

    Hey Gina,
    Thank you soooo much for this blog. I've been following it for quite some time now and I love it. I made this last night for dinner except I used fresh spinach and seasoned the chicken with Italian seasoning instead of pepper and salt. Went over great with my boyfriend. We are both watching what we eat and how many calories we take in. Paired it with a nice fresh spinach salad. So yummy. I love your blog and cant wait to make more. I'm making the baked potato soup tonight.

    Thanks again!

  64. Holly Drdsgirl — October 13, 2011 @ 8:19 pm Reply

    This is the second thing i made, and it was mouth watering. so YUMMY!! even hubby loved it.
    Thank u for this one it's a keeper!!!

  65. Gina @ Skinnytaste — August 2, 2011 @ 12:55 am Reply

    Thanks Barb, enjoy!

  66. BklynBarb — August 1, 2011 @ 5:57 pm Reply

    My SIL turned me on to your site. This is the first thing I made and there is only one word to describe this dish – YUMMY. The only thing I did different was I marinaded the chicken cutlets in a little lemon juice, olive oil, garlic and parsley for a couple of hours. All the tastes blended together were just perfect. Tonight I'm trying the Chicken and Mushrooms in the Garlic Wine Sauce. My mouth is already watering. Thank you for sharing all these wonderful recipes.

  67. Grazielle — June 30, 2011 @ 6:41 pm Reply

    This was great! I made it last night. We loved every bite.

  68. Maria — June 29, 2011 @ 1:26 am Reply

    This was incredible! Thanks as always!

  69. Anonymous — June 8, 2011 @ 8:54 pm Reply

    Made this and it was good ! YUMMY

  70. Laura — June 7, 2011 @ 1:19 am Reply

    just made this for dinner tonight! DELICIOUS. only difference we made was instead of roasted red peppers we used a roasted red pepper and artichoke tapenade from trader joe's. will be making this again! thanks!

  71. Andrea Collier — June 4, 2011 @ 2:21 am Reply

    Mmmmm…just made this for dinner. I only had a few points left for dinner and I made it with the roasted garlic broccoli! Both were delicious and my picky husband even had two servings! Thank you Thank you!

  72. Gina @ Skinnytaste — May 30, 2011 @ 2:35 am Reply

    It's the raw weight.

  73. Anonymous — May 29, 2011 @ 4:09 pm Reply

    Is this 24 oz raw or cooked? If cooked, how do you know how much raw before including all the ingredient on top?

  74. Beth O — May 15, 2011 @ 8:34 pm Reply

    Delicious! Another winner! My family (especially my husband) loves all the recipes I've tried. Thanks!

  75. Gina @ Skinnytaste — May 3, 2011 @ 2:30 pm Reply

    So happy you all liked it!

  76. Barb K — May 3, 2011 @ 1:57 pm Reply

    Love Love Love this recipe!! I've been a "fan" for a long time, but had never tried the recipes. This was my first! I can't wait to try more! Thank you so much!!

  77. Jess — April 30, 2011 @ 10:34 pm Reply

    This recipe is wonderful. I even substituted the red peppers with sliced tomatoes and mushrooms.

  78. Jenn — April 23, 2011 @ 11:22 am Reply

    This was great! I used feta cheese instead of mozzarella though. Super flavorful and it was so pretty to look at!

  79. Mary G — April 18, 2011 @ 2:15 am Reply

    Such a great idea! I made this using leftover grilled chicken and it was delicious! Thanks for all of your great recipes, you have honestly changed my outlook of weight watchers (I was getting bored eating the same things over and over).

  80. Anonymous — April 17, 2011 @ 10:20 pm Reply

    Love this…I used fresh spinach and it was so good.

  81. Gina @ Skinnytaste — April 16, 2011 @ 6:35 pm Reply

    Great idea using fish!

  82. swimmermama — April 16, 2011 @ 4:27 pm Reply

    I just wanted to let people know that I made this last night with tilapia (I am pescatarian). It was great! My husband, who rarely compliments my cooking since he really is the chef of the family, even liked it! And, again, it was so easy. Thank you for the tasty, healthy, and quick idea!

  83. Kathryn — April 13, 2011 @ 12:42 am Reply

    My boyfriend and I just finished eating this. Amazing! I used fresh spinach, super easy to throw in a pan while the chicken in cooking on the Forman.

    Love your recipes! Will be trying more of them this week!

  84. Gina @ Skinnytaste — April 12, 2011 @ 2:01 am Reply

    Glad you liked it Jennifer

  85. Jennifurla — April 12, 2011 @ 12:53 am Reply

    Just made this, loved it. I used baby spinach and sauteed it with the garlic. Added a wee flour coating to the chicken with cayenne.

    Thanks for the recipe Gina.

  86. Gina @ Skinnytaste — April 11, 2011 @ 4:18 pm Reply

    Dispatches, it's a meal in one for me, but a side of pasta like orzo would be great.

    Kassie, to cut the chicken breast, press down on the chicken with your hand then using your other hand take a sharp butcher knife and slice through. If you still can't do this, you can use thin pre cut cutlets. It's really a simple dish.

  87. Kassie — April 10, 2011 @ 4:40 pm Reply

    This may sound ridiculous, but I am giving up on trying this recipe. It seemed easy to me, too, but I can't seem to cut a chicken breast in half evenly (or even CLOSE!) Could I just use smaller breasts if I find them? Also, maybe once the grill is out and ready to go it will work, but doing the George Foreman, stovetop for the spinach, AND the broiler is too much for me. Don't get me wrong, I am a BIG FAN. Probably 3/4 of the meals I make are "Gina recipes". But I just can't seem to get this one to work out for me. Disappointed because it looks delish!

    • Unknown — April 10, 2013 @ 12:04 am Reply

      Don't give up!! There are videos on youtube you can watch. I couldn't figure it out at first too, but the videos helped and so does a sharp knife!

  88. Dispatches from Montana — April 6, 2011 @ 4:37 am Reply

    what would you suggest for the side with this dish thinking of making it this week.

  89. Anonymous — April 6, 2011 @ 2:39 am Reply

    This was simply delicious!!! I made it with fresh spinach and lots of garlic…Thanks for another winner recipe..Lyn

  90. Gina @ Skinnytaste — April 5, 2011 @ 6:58 pm Reply

    Laurie, drool!! Yum!!

    You guys are making me hungry!

  91. Javagal — April 5, 2011 @ 3:43 am Reply

    I made this with arugula instead of spinach and it was AMAZING!!!!

  92. 5d0303a6-5f1e-11e0-a3ec-000f20980440 — April 5, 2011 @ 12:48 am Reply

    This was a great meal that was healthy and full of flavor!!! Thanks so much!!

  93. Laurie — April 4, 2011 @ 11:31 pm Reply

    Prepared this today for dinner – it was terrific! I made a slight change. After I arranged it in the baking pan, I drizzled white wine and chicken broth over it. A very big success! Thank you!!!

  94. Jenn @leftoverqueen — April 4, 2011 @ 10:21 pm Reply

    Those look so delicious! Colorful too!

  95. Joanne — April 3, 2011 @ 12:00 am Reply

    I just made this for dinner using kale instead of spinach. It was delicious!

  96. Anonymous — April 2, 2011 @ 7:54 pm Reply

    Every recipe i have tried has been SO GOOD and i love it that the ww points are there too. Great Job!

  97. Wrena — April 2, 2011 @ 4:42 pm Reply

    Taking this to pot luck! Several of us are glutin free so I pike to take tasty stuff with no guessing game behind it. And it won't trip me up on by "diet" 🙂 ps I made a special button on my iPone just for your blog

  98. Daria @ Mom in Management — April 2, 2011 @ 3:59 pm Reply

    Yum! I'm making it right now. 🙂

  99. Gina @ Skinnytaste — April 2, 2011 @ 12:23 pm Reply

    Nikki, thanks for sharing! I had no idea there were vegetarian chicken cutlets!

  100. Nikki — April 2, 2011 @ 3:43 am Reply

    I just made this with Quorn's "Chik'n" Cutlets — Vegetarian 'chicken', that is — and it went over extremely well! Even my meat-eating boyfriend thought it was fantastic.

    Thanks for the terrific recipe. 🙂

  101. Laura — April 1, 2011 @ 2:48 am Reply

    I made this tonight and it was great! I didn't have mozzarella on hand, but I needed to polish off the blue cheese in the fridge – turned out great. Thanks for posting, Gina!

  102. Andrea @ strawberry-chic — April 1, 2011 @ 1:27 am Reply

    I'm always looking for new dressing recipes, especially on the light side…thanks for the idea!

  103. Maranda — March 31, 2011 @ 10:22 pm Reply

    This looks delicious! Though I feel as though I would need to add mushrooms to mine. Hmmmm….

  104. swimmermama — March 31, 2011 @ 9:27 pm Reply

    Our oven is broken 🙁

    As soon as it is fixed, I am trying this with tilapia! I love that your recipes almost never call for anything that we do not already have in our kitchen. It makes trying new recipes so easy and fun!

  105. Caveman Home Companion — March 31, 2011 @ 6:49 pm Reply

    This looks very tasty and easy enough that even I could cook it! Thanks!

  106. Laurie (Wally) — March 31, 2011 @ 6:39 pm Reply

    We made this last night… it was so yummy… the only thing we did not have was the roasted red pepers but it was tasty with out it… DH was really excited to have the leftovers for his lunch.. Thanks

  107. sprinkledwlove.com — March 31, 2011 @ 4:25 pm Reply

    Oh my goshhh looks divine! Can't wait to try this out.

    http://sprinkledwlove.com/

  108. Farmgirl Susan — March 31, 2011 @ 3:27 pm Reply

    YUM. So quick, so easy, so delish, and so ridiculous I never think to make something like this myself. Thanks for doing the thinking for us! 😉

  109. Gina @ Skinnytaste — March 31, 2011 @ 1:49 pm Reply

    Claire, sure why not!

    Thanks Zaklina!

  110. Zaklina — March 31, 2011 @ 1:08 pm Reply

    I can't wait to try this. I love your site!

  111. Diane S. — March 31, 2011 @ 11:40 am Reply

    Mouth watering dish! It really looks delicious. I think grilled pork will also be as delicious as this one when it is top with sauteed spinach with garlic, mozzarella cheese and roasted peppers! HHmm, Yummy!

  112. Claire @ Claire K Creations — March 31, 2011 @ 9:29 am Reply

    Oooh yum. Looks easy and delicious but I think it looks fancy enough for a casual dinner party too!

  113. Gina @ Skinnytaste — March 31, 2011 @ 3:08 am Reply

    Fresh sauteed baby spinach of course would be amazing if you prefer!!!

    For a fish, I would use a white fish like tilapia, flounder or sole. Great idea! Maybe add a little lemon to the spinach!

    Kelly, sure you could do this in a pan as well. Can't wait to hear your reviews.

  114. Healthy and Homemade — March 31, 2011 @ 2:54 am Reply

    You are a genius! That is all =)

  115. Kitchen with Kate — March 31, 2011 @ 2:36 am Reply

    This looks amazing! I just did something similar, it was a new and improved chicken florentine http://kitchenwithkate.blogspot.com/2011/03/throwback-thursday-new-and-improved.html This steps mine up a notch, I'm really looking forward to trying this. Thanks for sharing!

  116. Lynell — March 31, 2011 @ 2:33 am Reply

    Just Love you site! I shared it with Weight watchers FB page. Hope you don't mind.

  117. Jyl — March 30, 2011 @ 11:32 pm Reply

    What type of fish do you think would taste good with spinach and mozzarella, I have an allergy to chicken but its a great idea!

  118. Erin @TrialAndErin — March 30, 2011 @ 9:55 pm Reply

    YUMM! This looks so good Gina! I do believe I'll have to try this when I break in my new Staub grill pan!

  119. Kelly — March 30, 2011 @ 8:25 pm Reply

    Looks tasty. Though I may skip the grill pan and cook it in the skillet instead to save on dishes. I agree, no reason to eat boring ANYTHING.

  120. Danielle — March 30, 2011 @ 7:18 pm Reply

    Looks YUMMY – something else from you to add to my meal planner!

    We sing your graces nearly every night!

    Danielle

  121. Anonymous — March 30, 2011 @ 7:05 pm Reply

    Great idea for a healthy Italian meal! I put salsa and low fat Mexican cheese on grilled chicken for Mexican night!

  122. SamDIBO — March 30, 2011 @ 6:56 pm Reply

    Oh YUM! I *have* to make this now!

  123. Gillian — March 30, 2011 @ 6:54 pm Reply

    I will be making this over the weekend! It looks great.. I would assume there's no reason fresh spinach couldn't be used after wilting it on the stove w/the oil and garlic??

  124. Gina @ Skinnytaste — March 30, 2011 @ 6:41 pm Reply

    Don't you love how colorful it is? The colors of the Italian flag. Super easy, I actually made this for lunch today, took about 15 minutes start to finish!!

  125. Cerise — March 30, 2011 @ 6:40 pm Reply

    Yes please! That looks super yum!

  126. Anonymous — March 30, 2011 @ 6:32 pm Reply

    I was just introduced to your website and I am so happy! Love that you list the WW points plus! Cannot wait to start cooking and trying these recipies!!

  127. Elizabeth Ann (Elizabeth Ann's Recipe Box — March 30, 2011 @ 6:27 pm Reply

    ok this looks AMAZING! 🙂 AND I have two lonely chicken breasts in my freezer just BEGGING to be made into something DELICIOUS! 🙂

    • Resep Masakan — February 19, 2016 @ 3:17 am Reply

      I will be making this over the weekend! It looks great.. I would assume there's no reason fresh spinach couldn't be used after wilting it on the stove w/the oil and garlic??

  128. Julia — March 30, 2011 @ 6:26 pm Reply

    love how easy, healthy and beautiful this is!

Welcome!

Hi, I’m Gina Homolka, a busy mom of two girls, author and recipe developer here at Skinnytaste.com. My food philosophy is to eat seasonal, whole foods and maintain good portion control (everything in moderation!).
